topic_path: Estimating equations of lines of best fit, and using them to make predictions

8_SP.title: Interpret two-way tables
8_SP.description: Do you understand how two-way tables _really_ work? Here 's your chance to practice. Go get 'em! You got this!
topic_path: Using data displays to find associations

8_SP.title: Create two-way frequency tables
8_SP.description: Given a Venn diagram or information about a relationship, create a two-way frequency table.

8_SP.title: Estimating equations of lines of best fit, and using them to make predictions
8_SP.description: Practice estimating the equation of a line of best fit through data points in a scatter plot. Then, use the equation to make a prediction.
topic_path: Estimating equations of lines of best fit, and using them to make predictions

8_SP.title: Interpret two-way tables
8_SP.description: Do you understand how two-way tables _really_ work? Here 's your chance to practice. Go get 'em! You got this!

8_SP.title: Making appropriate scatter plots
8_SP.description: An appropriate scatter plot has the independent variable on the x-axis and the dependent variable on the y-axis. Also, the scale of both axes should be reasonable, making the data as easy to read as possible. In these practice problems, we practice telling good scatter plots from bad ones.

8_SP.title: Eyeballing the line of best fit
8_SP.description: Given a random assortment of points, draw a line of best fit through them.
topic_path: Eyeballing the line of best fit

8_SP.title: Making appropriate scatter plots
8_SP.description: An appropriate scatter plot has the independent variable on the x-axis and the dependent variable on the y-axis. Also, the scale of both axes should be reasonable, making the data as easy to read as possible. In these practice problems, we practice telling good scatter plots from bad ones.

8_SP.title: Describing trends in scatterplots
8_SP.description: Practice making sense of trends in scatterplots. That is, explain what trends mean in terms of real-world quantities.
topic_path: Observing more patterns in scatter plots

8_SP.title: Constructing scatter plots
8_SP.description: Practice plotting points to construct a scatter plot.

8_SP.title: Describing trends in scatterplots
8_SP.description: Practice making sense of trends in scatterplots. That is, explain what trends mean in terms of real-world quantities.

8_SP.title: Read two-way frequency tables
8_SP.description: Practice interpreting two-way frequency tables. For example, what does the cell in the top left tell you?

8_SP.title: Constructing scatter plots
8_SP.description: Practice plotting points to construct a scatter plot.

8_SP.title: Positive and negative linear associations from scatter plots
8_SP.description: Practice identifying the types of associations shown in scatter plots. Sometimes we see linear associations (positive or negative), sometimes we see non-linear associations (the data seems to follow a curve), and other times we don't see any association at all.
topic_path: Positive and negative linear associations from scatter plots

8_SP.title: Create two-way relative frequency tables
8_SP.description: Practice creating two-way relative frequency tables from a two-way frequency table.

HSA.title: Integration using completing the square
HSA.description: Find indefinite integrals of rational functions of the form 1/p(x) or 1/√p(x), where p(x) is a quadratic function, by completing the square in p(x).

HSA.title: Divide polynomials by linear expressions
HSA.description: Rewrite expressions of the form _**a(x)/b(x)**_ , where _**a**_ is a polynomial _**b**_ is a linear binomial, in the form _**q(x)+r(x)/b(x)**_ , where _**q**_ and _**r**_ are polynomials and the degree of _**r**_ is less than the degree of _**b**_.

HSA.description: Solve various problems that focus on the relationship between a^x=b and log_a(b)=x.

topic_path: Get ready for differentiation: definition and basic derivative rules
